Why guests love this hotel
The most recent 5-bubble reviews, displayed for hotels rated as very good or higher. See all reviews below for more information. Some reviews may have been automatically translated.
4.6
(1,932 reviews)
  • Kim V
    ·
    Apr 2026
    Hotel where people have thought of everything. The complete picture is correct to small details such as shoehorns, slippers,..... We stayed in the Corniche suite. Spacious room with views of the Nile. Soundproofed rooms. Nicely finished. The staff is very friendly and helpful. We also ate at restaurant 8, the Lebanese restaurant and the Italian restaurant. All 3 good of its kind. Breakfast is provided with all options. In short, highly recommended.
    Read more
  • Namikidori
    ·
    Mar 2026
    My review can only be excellent as this hotel met all our expectations. A geographical position overlooking the Nile , a reception hall and elegant bouquets that set the tone and sense of security felt as soon as the front door is passed. The welcome was very charming and very professional. The staff in all departments is of great discretion while being very efficient. We were lucky enough to be upgraded and our room had become a haven when we returned from our visiting days. Everything is well thought out and available, whether it be the spa , the pool or the restaurants, always the same charming welcome and this great professionalism. The rooms are extremely comfortable , spacious , elegant, with a large and functional bathroom. Cleanliness reigns everywhere and the absolutely complete and tasty breakfasts let you glimpse what the restaurant will be like. We dined several times at the Italian restaurant, and it was delicious. A special mention to the front desk supervisor who greatly contributed to making our stay most enjoyable. A hotel to recommend
    Read more
  • Sharon R
    ·
    Mar 2026
    After a full day at the Egyptian Museum we stopped into the Four Seasons hoping to find a table for dinner. We met Mr. Kamal Walid at the Concierge Desk who accompanied my wife and I to several of the hotels' restaurants searching for a table until he could finally procure us a table at the poolside restaurant and bar where we enjoyed a lovely meal and service. Our experience was incredible because of Mr. Walid. He is an astounding gentleman and representative o The Four Seasons, a true asset to the hotel. Because of him we would book our next stay in Cairo at The Four Seasons.
    Read more
  • giddypam
    ·
    Mar 2026
    This 4S is probably not as nice as many, but it’s still the gold standard in Cairo. We had a premium Nile view king for four nights and it was fine. Only complaint is a lack of horizontal space in the room—no place to put packing cubes, etc. Great location. Nice breakfast. Ate at the Italian resto on Valentines Day. There was a set menu which was a bit too much. Also ate at “8” the Asian-themed resto. Food was good, but service seemed a bit confused. Lobby bar is very nice, as is the bar above the pool area. Beware though . . . Cocktails and imported wine are extremely expensive. All-in-all a pleasant stay, if a bit underwhelming.
    Read more
  • Lee M
    ·
    Mar 2026
    A Truly World Class Hotel located ideally in Cairo. I can’t recommend this hotel enough. One of the finest I’ve visited anywhere in the World. Staff went out of their way to assist. I emailed a request through in English and Salma Ahmad answered swiftly enabling my wishes and everything was arranged with style and elegance. If you are looking for the best hotel in Cairo… Stay here.

Tripadvisor gives a Travelers’ Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor.
Nestled on the banks of the River Nile in the heart of downtown Cairo, Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza offers an unparalleled blend of luxury, comfort and breathtaking views. Our prestigious destination boasts world-class dining, an award-winning Spa, and elegantly designed rooms and suites with panoramic views of the city and the Nile river. Just moments away from our Hotel are Egypt's 5,000-year-old ancient wonders, including the Pyramids of Giza, Grand Egyptian Museum, Khan El Khalili market and 12th-century Citadel.

Reviews summary
This summary was created by AI, based on recent reviews.
Powered by AI
For many travelers, Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza stands out for its spacious, comfortable rooms and impressive views, although some mention aging decor and maintenance issues. The hotel's central location is popular, offering convenient access to attractions. While the service earns accolades for warmth and attention, occasional inconsistencies are noted. Amenities like the pool and dining options are praised, but some guests feel the costs are high and not always justified. Cleanliness and atmosphere generally receive positive feedback, enhancing the overall experience.

Namikidori wrote a review Mar 2026
Le Cannet, France278 contributions155 helpful votes
Absolute comfort
My review can only be excellent as this hotel met all our expectations. A geographical position overlooking the Nile , a reception hall and elegant bouquets that set the tone and sense of security felt as soon as the front door is passed. The welcome was very charming and very professional. The staff in all departments is of great discretion while being very efficient. We were lucky enough to be upgraded and our room had become a haven when we returned from our visiting days.
Everything is well thought out and available, whether it be the spa , the pool or the restaurants, always the same charming welcome and this great professionalism.
The rooms are extremely comfortable , spacious , elegant, with a large and functional bathroom. Cleanliness reigns everywhere and the absolutely complete and tasty breakfasts let you glimpse what the restaurant will be like.
We dined several times at the Italian restaurant, and it was delicious.
A special mention to the front desk supervisor who greatly contributed to making our stay most enjoyable.
A hotel to recommend

Sharon R wrote a review Mar 2026
Kirkwood, Missouri102 contributions30 helpful votes
Four Seasons Nile Plaza , Cairo, Egypt and Mr. Kamal Walid
After a full day at the Egyptian Museum we stopped into the Four Seasons hoping to find a table for dinner. We met Mr. Kamal Walid at the Concierge Desk who accompanied my wife and I to several of the hotels' restaurants searching for a table until he could finally procure us a table at the poolside restaurant and bar where we enjoyed a lovely meal and service.
Our experience was incredible because of Mr. Walid. He is an astounding gentleman and representative o The Four Seasons, a true asset to the hotel. Because of him we would book our next stay in Cairo at The Four Seasons.

Dear Sharon,

Thank you for sharing such a wonderful account of your visit. It truly warms our hearts to know that your evening after the Egyptian Museum became even more memorable.
Mr. Walid embodies the spirit of genuine care that guides everything we do, and your kind words will mean so much to him. We’re delighted that he was able to accompany you personally and ensure you found the perfect spot to enjoy your dinner by the pool. Moments like these are exactly what we hope to create for every guest who walks through our doors.
We would be honored to welcome you for a future stay, and we look forward to the opportunity to craft even more special experiences during your next visit to Cairo.

giddypam wrote a review Mar 2026
Fort Lauderdale, Florida62 contributions64 helpful votes
Expectations Met, But Underwhelmed
This 4S is probably not as nice as many, but it’s still the gold standard in Cairo. We had a premium Nile view king for four nights and it was fine. Only complaint is a lack of horizontal space in the room—no place to put packing cubes, etc. Great location. Nice breakfast. Ate at the Italian resto on Valentines Day. There was a set menu which was a bit too much. Also ate at “8” the Asian-themed resto. Food was good, but service seemed a bit confused. Lobby bar is very nice, as is the bar above the pool area. Beware though . . . Cocktails and imported wine are extremely expensive. All-in-all a pleasant stay, if a bit underwhelming.

Response from Four Seasons C, Marketing at Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza
Responded 3 weeks ago
Dear Giddypam,

Thank you for taking the time to share such a thoughtful and detailed review of your recent stay with us. While I’m glad to know that you enjoyed our central location, Nile views, breakfast offering, and the atmosphere of our lounges, I’m truly sorry to learn that certain elements of your visit fell short of the seamless experience we strive to deliver.

Your comments regarding the layout and available space in your guest room are very helpful, and I appreciate you bringing this to our attention. We continuously review our accommodations to ensure they are as comfortable and functional as possible, and your feedback will certainly be considered in our ongoing improvements.

I am also grateful for your notes on the dining experiences, from the set Valentine’s Day menu to the service at 8 Restaurant. While I’m pleased to hear you enjoyed the cuisine, I regret that the service felt anything less than intuitive. This is not reflective of the standard we aim to provide, and I will be reviewing your impressions with our restaurant teams to ensure greater consistency and clarity.

Regarding pricing of imported beverages, I understand how this may have impacted your impression. We do our best to balance quality, availability, and guest experience, and your perspective is invaluable as we assess these offerings.

Thank you once again for your honest insight. I’m pleased that your stay was pleasant overall, but I sincerely hope we have the opportunity to welcome you back and deliver a visit that feels truly exceptional in every way.

Kind regards,
Yves Giacometti
Regional Vice President and General Manager
Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza
This response is the subjective opinion of the management representative and not of Tripadvisor LLC nor of its partners.

sandy s wrote a review Feb 2026
London, United Kingdom69 contributions
See all 5 photos
Four Seasons 5 star- absolutely not.
5 star Four Seasons- this does not deliver anywhere near a 5 star experience.
At the beginning of a 5 night stay I was thinking of 4 star recommendation- this changed rapidly.
Breakfast service is best described as ‘chaotic’ and I struggle to give any positives. We were left to stand and wait by an uncleared table on every visit- table was not set up even after waiting. Guests were collecting their own cutlery from a disorganised service. One morning after waiting for a table- we came back from the buffet to find our table ‘given away’, even though we had coffee and juice in place. If you wanted a plate to get some pastries- they never existed unless you asked.
Room service was really below par to be honest. Bedding was made- upside down- I’ll post a photo. We came back after a day exploring at 3.10 and no room service. Room was as we left it. I contacted room service, I was told that that it’s up to 3pm that rooms are s serviced. The duvets are very old and lumpy.
Our balcony door- room 2811- had issues from the start. The handle came off and we reported this twice. A maintenance person came to fix it and we went out leaving him. We came back and the whole mechanism fell out- to be fair, we were offered another room, but we were checking out the following day. We slept with an insecure door. The furniture on the balcony is dated.
To sum up what other guests have commented on-
Reception staff are great. The other staff are talking in groups or on their phones. No concierge help outside the hotel, no showing to your room- you find your own way and are expected to figure out lights etc.
Insider tip:
I think that the 5 star hotels in Cairo know that you visit for the Pyramids- they don’t rely on repeat custom.
Date of stay:
February 2026
Trip type:
Traveled as a couple

Response from Four Seasons C, Marketing at Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza
Responded Feb 26, 2026
Dear Sandy,

Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback. I am truly sorry to learn that your stay did not reflect the level of comfort, service, and attention to detail that we are committed to delivering. Your experience is deeply concerning and certainly not representative of our standards.

Please accept my sincere apologies for the inconvenience and frustration you encountered. While I am pleased to know that our reception team made a positive impression, it is clear that we fell short in providing the seamless and attentive experience you rightfully expected throughout the hotel. Your comments have been shared with our leadership team for immediate review and corrective action, as we are committed to addressing these shortcomings and improving our guests’ experiences.

We value your candid feedback, as it helps us identify where we must do better. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss your experience further and personally restore your confidence in our hotel.

Kind regards,
Yves Giacometti
Regional Vice President and General Manager

TomG20 wrote a review Feb 2026
Bern, Switzerland278 contributions166 helpful votes
Grandeur, History and Unmatched Service on the Nile
Our stay at Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza was outstanding in every way.
The hotel is impressively large, yet it never feels impersonal. Instead, it combines grandeur with elegance and a sense of history that gives the entire property a very special atmosphere.

The team delivers service at the highest level – attentive, warm and genuinely dedicated to making every moment seamless. You feel valued from arrival to departure.

The rooms are spacious and extremely comfortable, with wonderful views over the Nile and the city skyline. Waking up to that panorama alone makes the stay memorable.

An exceptional address in Cairo – I would return anytime without hesitation.

Денис wrote a review Feb 2026
19 contributions1 helpful vote
See all 6 photos
Rest Four Seasons Hotel Cairo 13.02-16.02.26
Were in this hotel from 13.02-16.02.2026
The room was a Diplomatic Suite (bedroom + living room).
Pros
1. Chic view - probably from certain rooms (we had a Diplomatic Suite )
2. Good breakfasts + dinners in an Egyptian restaurant
There's no upside to that!
Cons
1. Hotel well very old
2. Smoked just everything, even near the elevator there are ashtrays (non-smokers will be very uncomfortable )
3. Dropped in at 13.02 towards the evening - floor 23. Stayed in the room for about an hour, moved to the 14th floor of the room. The smell of paint hits your head to the point of vomiting. It's the same story in the lobby where the rooms are located. It feels like they just finished the renovation. We were told that the air conditioner broke )). On the 14th floor with smells everything is ok, but this room had non-functioning light switches, almost got electrocuted (photo attached ).
4. Without a deposit on the room, you cannot write anything down (it is not clear why I will still make payment at check-out, in other hotels with this problem )
5. In the restaurant there are constant problems with change - especially if you pay in dollars (you will wait a very long time, they go to change through an ATM ). And that's with me tipping zero loyalty every time.
6. Very noisy even with the windows closed tc near the road (look at the map all understand ), the buzzing auto just doesn't stop (slept constantly with earplugs ). By the way were in the hotel opposite Sofitel Cairo Nile - there is no such problem the road runs at the back of the hotel, and the waterfront they have their own with restaurant and pier.

SB19792013 wrote a review Feb 2026
Leicester, United Kingdom5 contributions6 helpful votes
Wonderful Hotel
Beautiful hotel, very clean with amazing staff who cannot do enough for you.

The rooms are elegant and with fresh flowers, clean linens and towels.

The restaurants on site and over the road offer exceptional food of international cuisine.

The pool area is clean and relaxing and well attended by caring staff.

The staff have been the best part of the hotel for me - they work very hard to ensure your stay is enjoyable.

Finally, the sites nearby are awesome inspiring and magical. The pyramids and museums are breathtaking and security has been massively increased particularly for foreign visitors.

The only thing that has been upsetting to see, which is not the fault of the hotel, is how some of the horses and other animals are treated near the pyramids. I urge visitors not to ride the horses or camels or take carriage rides as the animals are treated very poorly, they are carrying loads that far exceed their abilities and they are visibly thin. Please Cairo, you have done so much for us tourists and your local economy, please, please increase animal welfare too.
Insider tip:
Avoid horse/ camel riding, carriages etc. the welfare of the animals is bad
Date of stay:
February 2026
Trip type:
Traveled as a couple
This review is the subjective opinion of an individual traveler and not of Tripadvisor LLC nor of its partners. Tripadvisor performs checks on reviews as part of our industry-leading trust & safety standards. Read our transparency report to learn more.
Response from Four Seasons C, Marketing at Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza
Responded 3 weeks ago
Dear Valued Guest,

Thank you for sharing such a wonderful and detailed review. I’m delighted to hear how much you enjoyed the elegance of our rooms, the care of our team, and the variety of dining options both at the hotel and just steps away. It’s also lovely to know that our pool area and our colleagues’ warm hospitality helped make your stay so memorable.

Your reflections on Cairo’s iconic sites are beautifully expressed, and I appreciate your thoughtful note about animal welfare around the pyramids. While this is outside the hotel’s control, your compassion is truly admirable, and I thank you for sharing this perspective with fellow travelers.
We hope to have the pleasure of welcoming you back for another relaxing and inspiring stay in Cairo.

Kind regards,
Yves Giacometti
Regional Vice President and General Manager
Four Seasons Hotel Cairo at Nile Plaza

This is the version of our website addressed to speakers of English in the United States. If you are a resident of another country or region, please select the appropriate version of Tripadvisor for your country or region in the drop-down menu.



Tripadvisor LLC makes no guarantees for availability of prices advertised on our sites and applications. Listed prices may require a stay of a particular length or have blackout dates, qualifications or other applicable restrictions. Tripadvisor LLC is not responsible for any content on external web sites that are not owned or operated by Tripadvisor . Indicative hotel prices displayed on our “Explore” pages are estimates extrapolated from historic pricing data.



Tripadvisor LLC is not a booking agent or tour operator. When you book with one of our partners, please be sure to check their site for a full disclosure of all applicable fees.



Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.
Privacy and Cookies Statement

Manage Consent Preferences

Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms.    You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

Performance Cookies

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site.    All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

Functional Cookies

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages.    If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Targeting Cookies

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites.    They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
